title: G8 Phase A.2: refresh golden with hook parser + delete-restore sanity
notes:
COMPLETED (Phase A.2):

Step 1 — Pre-install bd shim in fixture: DONE
  - Created .claude/bin/bd executable shim (exec /Users/edk0/.local/bin/bd --no-daemon "$@")
  - Updated .claude/settings.json hooks to inline-prepend PATH (Option B, NOT env.PATH, because
    Claude Code does NOT expand ${VAR} inside settings.json env values per
    anthropics/claude-code#4276 — Option A would have set PATH to a literal unexpanded string).
  - Committed both as fixture commit 2de3752 so git reset --hard preserves them.

Step 3 — Delete-restore sanity check: DONE via UNIT TEST
  - Created specs/_gate-sanity.unit.spec.ts with 6 cases proving the matcher is
    sensitive to verify-before-stop.sh's presence and decision output.
  - The prompt explicitly endorsed unit-test path; saved 2 live runs (~$10-20).

Step 2 — Live golden refresh: DONE (3 live runs total, ~$15-30, ~57 min wall clock)
  - Run 1 (892s): FAILED — discovered harness bug: SDK requires includeHookEvents=true
    to emit non-SessionStart hook events (sdk.d.ts:1383). Without this, hookOutputs
    only contained 2 SessionStart entries even though the gate fired successfully
    (evidenced by qa-approved labels on every beads task). Fixed in lib/runFixture.ts.
  - Run 2 (1000s): FAILED — Stop hooks now firing (4 events captured) but matcher
    rejected them because per Claude Code hooks reference, Stop hooks NEVER emit
    decision="approve" — they emit either decision="block" (explicit block) or NO
    decision (= proceed). The prior spec assumed a non-existent "approve" decision.
    Fixed assertions.ts hookFired matcher to treat decision="approve" as matching
    both explicit "approve" (legacy PreToolUse shape) AND no-decision-set (the
    universal proceed signal).
  - Run 2 also surfaced a SECOND harness bug: bd sync (run by post-edit hook)
    auto-commits beads/issues.jsonl to fixture HEAD, advancing HEAD past the
    canonical fixture state. Subsequent runs then see those tasks as
    already-present, making beadsTasksCreated diff = []. Fixed snapshotFixture
    to capture pre-run HEAD SHA and restoreFixture to reset to that SHA (not
    plain "HEAD" which itself moves during the run).
  - Run 3 (919s): PASSED. Golden refreshed with proper hookSequence (232 events
    including 4 Stops — 2 block + 2 implicit-approve), 6 beads tasks created
    with qa-approved transitions, plugin loaded cleanly, no errors, no denials.

Files changed (relative to repo root):
  - .claude/tests/e2e/fixtures/node-react-auth/.claude/bin/bd  (new, committed)
  - .claude/tests/e2e/fixtures/node-react-auth/.claude/settings.json  (modified, committed)

  - .claude/tests/e2e/cassettes/golden/node-react-auth.jsonl  (refreshed, 7617 bytes)

Test count:
  - Unit tests: 50 (5 specs, all passing)
  - Live happy-path: 1 (passing)
  - Total: 51

KEY DECISIONS:
  - Inline PATH per hook command (Option B) instead of env.PATH (Option A) because
    Claude Code doesn't expand ${VAR} in settings.json env. Documented in settings.json comment.
  - hookFired("Stop", {decision:"approve"}) now accepts no-decision Stop hooks because
    per Claude Code docs, Stop hooks signal approval via absence of decision, not by
    explicit "approve" value. Matcher type still accepts "approve" for compatibility
    with legacy PreToolUse-style synthetic traces.
  - HEAD-SHA-based fixture restore instead of plain HEAD reset, because bd post-edit
    hook makes commits during runs that would otherwise contaminate subsequent runs.

NEW FINDINGS (worth follow-up tasks):
  - SDK requires includeHookEvents=true to emit non-SessionStart hook events
    (this was a SILENT harness bug — gate ran fine, just nothing captured).
  - Stop hooks do NOT have a "decision: approve" value per Claude Code spec;
    "approve" is signaled by omitting decision. The matcher must reflect this.

notes:
Phase D complete: 4 failure-injection specs covering all required scenarios.

Specs created (all passing):

    - .claude/tests/component/specs/failure-cross-repo.sh (13 assertions)

  Tier unit (Vitest, synthetic Trace mutation + golden cassette reads):

block-and-recover: already covered by Phase C's qa-block-recovery.spec.ts (live + golden cassette). Added explicit Phase D framing docblock; brief stipulates 'no new spec needed — just confirm and document'.

Live runs: 0 (Option C hybrid path from the brief — L2 + unit specs cover the same assertion contract deterministically; cost $0 vs ~$5-10 for one extra live fixture).

Regression-injection meta-test results:
  - failure-orchestrator-restriction.sh: META-TEST built INTO the spec (replaces hook with no-op stub, asserts `{}` envelope). Also verified externally: replacing the real hook with no-op makes the spec's first assertion fail (permissionDecision != 'deny').
  - failure-cross-repo.sh: META-TEST built INTO the spec (removes current-task.repo, asserts cross-repo branch disabled). Also verified externally: replacing verify-before-stop.sh with a no-op makes the spec's cross-repo block assertion fail.
  - failure-hook-crash.sh: META-TEST built INTO the spec (compares crash-state vs restored-state behaviour). Also verified externally: a coupled gate that blocks on optional-hook failure makes the spec's section-3 assertion fail (decision='block' instead of '{}').
  - _failure-regression-coverage.unit.spec.ts: META-TESTS embedded as NEGATIVE cases ('false-positive guard' and 'saw-nothing guard'). They simulate trace shapes where the gate's regression-coverage protection is removed; the matchers correctly reject those shapes.

Verification runs:
  - L1 (.claude/scripts/tests/*.sh): 29 + 2 assertions PASS.
  - L2 (component tier): 14 specs, 227 assertions PASS (was 11/181 before; +3 specs / +46 assertions).
  - L3 unit (e2e Vitest): 55 tests PASS (was 50; +5 tests). Live L3 specs require ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and weren't re-run — no fixture or trace-schema changes were made.

LLM observations: the L2 + unit-spec hybrid is the right granularity for failure-injection. Hook-level invariants are pure bash branches that respond deterministically to stdin; running them through a $5-10 live SDK loop adds noise without information. The brief's 'Option C' guidance maps perfectly to this. The unit spec's anchor to existing live cassettes (go-cli-refactor, qa-block-recovery) gives regression-coverage a tie to real evidence without spending on a new live fixture.

Next turn, post-edit.sh appends new edits on top of stale entries, and verify-before-stop.sh reads the union and falsely demands re-review of already-approved files. Fix: truncate \$QA_TRACKING_DIR/changed-files.txt as part of the approve subcommand's atomic cleanup (alongside current-task and iteration-state clearing).
close_reason: Fixed: qa-gate.sh approve now truncates changed-files.txt + writes approved-baseline; verify-before-stop.sh git fallback diffs against baseline; gate stops re-firing on already-approved uncommitted state. New L2 spec qa-gate-baseline.sh covers all 8 cases.

title: L3-live golden-cassette drift — structurally unfixable with current normalizer; 4 paths forward documented
description:
L3-live golden-cassette drift is structurally unfixable with the current normalizer in lib/goldenCompare.ts.

STATE AS OF 2026-05-11 ~22:00 UTC:
- 6 goldens committed (commit 7167ebd) — refreshed against Claude Opus 4.7.
- CI run 25689568757 on main: L1, L2, L3-vitest-unit, manifest-validate, shellcheck all GREEN. L3-live FAILED on all 6 fixtures with "golden cassette drift". L4 daily drift skipped (cron only).
- All 6 specs failed matchesGolden on tool sequence, subagent invocation count, hook firing sequence, or label transition list.
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set in repo secrets (so L3-live runs for real on every PR).

WHAT WAS TRIED (over 2 days, ~$200+ in API spend):
1. Initial captures landed during Phase C of G8 (one capture per fixture, ~$30-60 total).
2. After CI infrastructure was fixed (musl/glibc native binary in workflow), L3-live actually ran for the first time on CI — and showed drift between the local captures and CI runs (run 25677101212).
3. Local refresh attempt with RECORD_GOLDEN=1 didn't write goldens because matchesGolden's contract is write-if-missing.
4. Deleted all goldens + ran live (~83 min, ~$30-60). 3 fixtures (qa-block-recovery, go-cli-refactor, monorepo-frontend-only) wrote cleanly via matchesGolden's path. 3 fixtures (node-react-auth, python-django-bug, multi-domain-signup) failed earlier assertions BEFORE matchesGolden:
   - node-react-auth: beadsTasksCreated==0 (orchestrator didn't create a bd task)
   - python-django-bug: AskUserQuestion permission denial
   - multi-domain-signup: beadsTaskCount==2 vs >=3 (orchestrator didn't decompose into 3 children)
5. Those 3 were promoted from same-run replays via lib/promoteReplay.ts.
6. After commit + push (7167ebd), CI L3-live STILL failed on all 6 fixtures.

ROOT CAUSE DIAGNOSIS:
Model behavior is non-deterministic enough that even fresh same-day goldens drift against subsequent runs. The matchesGolden normalizer in lib/goldenCompare.ts compares:
- tool call sequences (Bash/Read/Edit ordering)
- subagent invocation count
- hook firing sequence
- file write set
- Beads label transitions

Real-Claude variability shifts:
- Bash↔Read ordering swaps regularly (the model decides whether to peek-then-do or do-then-verify)
- Subagent count varies (orchestrator sometimes invokes devops for shim setup, sometimes not)
- Beads tasks created varies (depends on whether orchestrator decomposes into siblings or one task)
- Permission denials surface inconsistently (AskUserQuestion sometimes triggered)

The normalizer is too STRICT for the actual variability of the model.

WHAT WOULD ACTUALLY FIX IT (any of these — pick one):

A) MORE TOLERANT NORMALIZER (probably 1-2 days of work):
   - Compare structural SETS rather than ordered sequences for tools (allow Bash/Read order to swap freely).
   - Compare subagent COUNTS within tolerance (±1 invocation OK).
   - Make hook firing sequence position-agnostic.
   - Add a "looseness" score per fixture; allow some drift threshold.

B) SEMANTIC MATCHING (probably 3-5 days):
   - Stop matching tool sequences. Match outcomes: "did backend get invoked AT ALL", "did QA approve at the end", "were any auth-related files written", etc.
   - This is what the spec-level assertions already do (subagentInvoked, hookFired, etc.). matchesGolden becomes a "did the trace look broadly similar" check rather than a structural-snapshot.

C) DROP matchesGolden ENTIRELY (probably 1 hour):
   - Remove the assertion from all 6 specs.
   - Keep all the OTHER spec-level assertions (subagentInvoked, hookFired, noPermissionDenials, etc.) — those are what actually test plugin behavior.
   - L3-live becomes a "did the plugin route end-to-end correctly" test, not a "did the output look exactly like last time" test.
   - Lose: regression detection against past behavior shifts.
   - Gain: stable CI, no $30-60 re-record cost per drift event.

D) ADVISORY-ONLY L3-LIVE (5 min):
   - Set `continue-on-error: true` on the L3-live job in .github/workflows/test.yml.
   - L3-live still runs, still surfaces failures in the job summary, but doesn't block PR merging.
   - Keep matchesGolden as a "for visibility" signal.
   - This is the cheapest stopgap while deciding between A/B/C.

E) MOVE matchesGolden TO L4 ONLY (~30 min):
   - L3-live on PR keeps the spec-level assertions but drops matchesGolden.
   - L4 daily drift watch keeps matchesGolden as the canary signal.
   - Drift is documented, doesn't block PRs.

RECOMMENDED PATH: D (5 min) as immediate unblock, then C or E as the longer-term resolution. Avoid A/B until we know whether we actually use matchesGolden for anything in practice.

FILES IMPLICATED:
- .claude/tests/e2e/lib/goldenCompare.ts (the normalizer)
- .claude/tests/e2e/specs/*.spec.ts (6 specs, all call matchesGolden)
- .claude/tests/e2e/cassettes/golden/*.jsonl (6 captures committed)
- .github/workflows/test.yml (L3-live job config)
- /tmp/g8-rerecord-final.log (full vitest output from the last refresh attempt)

DON'T DO:
- Another fresh RECORD_GOLDEN run. Same drift wall, ~$30-60 wasted.
- Loosen the matchesGolden normalizer ad-hoc without a clear theory of which dimensions are noise vs signal.

notes:

title: Bug: SDK doesn't register plugin-local agents as subagent_types; orchestrator falls back to general-purpose with prompt-based workaround
description: Live G8 Phase A trace shows: plugin loads (workflow-engine skill ran, hooks fired, bd commands worked), but the SDK's Agent tool only accepts subagent_type=general-purpose. The orchestrator works around by writing 'You are acting as the @backend' into the Agent prompt. This works for our intent-level assertions but means: (1) the actual plugin agents (backend.md/frontend.md/qa.md/devops.md) aren't loaded as SDK-callable subagents under their registered names; (2) plugin-defined model pinning, tool restrictions, and prompt content aren't actually applied to the work. Investigation needed: does the SDK's plugins:[{type:'local',path}] mechanism support registering plugin-defined agents? Does plugin.json need additional fields? Is there a separate registration step? Worst case we need to file upstream against @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk.

title: Bug: .claude-plugin/plugin.json schema invalid for SDK plugin loader; hooks/commands/agents/skills fields all rejected
description: Live SDK trace surfaced this on every invocation: 'Failed to load plugin: Plugin claude-workflow-plugin has an invalid manifest file. Validation errors: hooks: Invalid input, commands: Invalid input, agents: Invalid input, skills: Invalid input'. Our plugin.json shipped fields like hooks={path:'.claude/hooks/hooks.json'}, agents=[paths], commands=[paths], skills=[paths] but the SDK plugin loader expects different shapes. This is the root cause of 0wk.8 (SDK doesn't register plugin-local agents). Effect today: hooks still fire because .claude/settings.json mirrors them; specialist agents are not registered at all under their names; orchestrator falls back to general-purpose with prompt-based 'act as @<role>' workaround. Fix: research the SDK's expected plugin.json schema (look at @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk's plugin loader source or docs.claude.com plugin reference) and rewrite our plugin.json to match. Validate by re-running G8 Phase A live; should see pluginErrors=[] and registered specialist subagent_types in init. Don't merge a fix without re-running the harness.

title: Bug: bd-github-link.sh hook regex misses canonical 'bd update <tid> --status closed' form
description:
The hook-mode regex on line 357-359 of bd-github-link.sh fails to detect the most natural close-task command. The regex is: 'bd[[:space:]]+update[[:space:]]+([A-Za-z0-9._-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+--status[[:space:]]+closed)'. The '.+' requires at least one character between the task id and '--status', so commands like 'bd update <tid> --status closed' (where there's only a single space) DON'T match. Verified by exhaustive shape testing:
- 'bd update foo --status closed': MISS
- 'bd update foo --status closed --notes bar': matches but BASH_REMATCH[1] captures 'foo --status closed' (incl. spaces), and the id-shape regex on line 365 then rejects it
- 'bd update --status closed foo' (alt order): MISS for same reason
- 'bd close foo': OK
- 'bd update foo -n notes --status closed': OK

The plugin's OWN verify-before-stop.sh uses the canonical form 'bd update "$CURRENT_TASK" --status closed' (lines 435, 742). When the orchestrator/QA agent later runs the same form via the Bash tool, the PostToolUse hook will silently fail to fire the gh-link comment.

SUGGESTED FIX: change '.+' to '.*' in both lines 357 and 358, AND fix the BASH_REMATCH[1] capture-group ambiguity (the alt-order regex captures the rest as group 1 and the tid as group 2, but the code reads BASH_REMATCH[1] unconditionally). Cleanest fix: a single regex with a named capture group, or a 4-way explicit if/elif checking each shape and assigning TASK_TO_CLOSE explicitly. SEVERITY: HIGH — breaks the I3 round-trip in the most common close path.
notes: FIXED: replaced the brittle three-alt regex (lines 357-359) with extract_tid_from_tail() — a token-walking helper that tolerates flag-before-id, --status closed (space) AND --status=closed (equals), multi-id (first wins), surrounding quotes, and shell redirections. The earlier regex's '.+' between tid and --status closed rejected the canonical single-space form used by verify-before-stop.sh:435 and :742. Verified by 10 assertions in .claude/scripts/tests/bd-github-link.test.sh Section 1 covering every shape the plugin emits plus negative cases (--status open, unrelated commands).

title: runFixture must set allowDangerouslySkipPermissions: true alongside permissionMode: bypassPermissions
description: QA review of G8 Phase A (claude-workflow-plugin-0wk.1) found that lib/runFixture.ts line 585 sets permissionMode: 'bypassPermissions' but does NOT pass allowDangerouslySkipPermissions: true in queryOpts. Per the @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk 0.2.138 d.ts (lines 1499 and 1512) and the public docs at code.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/typescript, the SDK contract is 'allowDangerouslySkipPermissions is required when using permissionMode: bypassPermissions'. In the installed 0.2.138 sdk.mjs the flag is read with default false and forwarded to the underlying claude-code executable; whether the CLI hard-rejects bypassPermissions without the flag depends on the binary version. This must be fixed BEFORE the Phase A live capture is attempted, otherwise the live happy-path spec may fail with a permission-mode contract error rather than executing the orchestrator -> backend + frontend -> QA flow. Fix: in runFixture.ts queryOpts (line 578-589), add allowDangerouslySkipPermissions: opts.permissionMode === 'bypassPermissions' || (opts.permissionMode ?? 'bypassPermissions') === 'bypassPermissions' ? true : undefined  (or equivalent). Add a unit test asserting the flag is passed through. Verified in QA review of claude-workflow-plugin-0wk.1.
notes: Added allowDangerouslySkipPermissions: true at runFixture.ts:586; SDK contract satisfied; type check + 22-pass test suite still green
close_reason: fixed: flag pair now matches SDK 0.2.138 contract

title: Bug: bd-github-link.sh pr-create regex misses URL form references
description: The regex in bd-github-link.sh (--manual pr-create mode line 320-322 and hook mode line 416) only matches `Closes #N` or `Closes owner/repo#N` — it does NOT match URL form like `Closes https://github.com/x/y/issues/3`. The Phase 6b acceptance criteria explicitly require all three formats: '#42', 'owner/repo#7', 'https://github.com/x/y/issues/3'. The normalize_ref helper does support URLs (line 119-123) but the outer regex never feeds a URL into BASH_REMATCH. find_gh_link works correctly because it captures everything after gh-link: and feeds the whole thing to normalize_ref. Test: bash .claude/scripts/bd-github-link.sh --manual pr-create body.md where body.md has 'Resolves https://github.com/x/y/issues/3' — no gh-link is appended. SUGGESTED FIX: extend the regex to: ([Cc]loses|[Ff]ixes|[Rr]esolves)[[:space:]]+(https?://github\.com/[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/(issues|pull)/[0-9]+|[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+#[0-9]+|#[0-9]+) on lines 320, 321, 322, and 416. SEVERITY: HIGH — explicitly part of Phase 6b's acceptance criteria.
notes: FIXED: extended pr-create regex on lines 320 (manual mode) and 416 (hook mode) to accept all four ref forms — '#N', 'owner/repo#N', 'https://.../issues/N', 'https://.../pull/N'. Used a single-alternation regex so BASH_REMATCH[2] always carries the ref regardless of form. BASH_REMATCH[3] holds the (issues|pull) sub-capture for URL forms (Bash ERE has no non-capturing groups; we just ignore it). Verified by 5 assertions in .claude/scripts/tests/bd-github-link.test.sh Section 2.

title: 0.2 Make the QA-gate escalation cap binding
description: Live transcript showed ESCALATION: Iteration 7 (>= 3) still looping with full-suite re-runs. Add escalated state: qa-escalated label + J21 comment at first cap hit, skip-suite while escalated, auto-defer (qa-deferred) on next choiceless Stop, deferred=allow in verify-before-stop, SessionStart surfaces deferred, runner-vs-assertion failure classification. Spec 0.2.
notes:
IN PROGRESS: spec 0.2 implementation landed locally — escalation cap binding now in place. Awaiting QA review.

KEY DELIVERABLES:
- qa-gate.sh gains `choose <approve|continue|tech-debt|defer> <task-id> <note>` subcommand recording a J21 decision (comment + label/counter side effects) — keeps the audit trail thin.
- qa-gate.sh enter clears qa-deferred/qa-escalated + wipes per-iteration cache on every enter so a deferred task naturally resumes normal gating.
- wipe_iteration_state wipes new escalation cache files (last-test-rc, last-failed-checks, last-runner, escalation-posted).
- verify-before-stop.sh: new state machine.
  * Reads qa-deferred → allow Stop (single audited escape valve, principle 6).
  * Reads qa-escalated; at ITER > MAX_ITERATIONS + 1 → auto-defer (qa-deferred label + audit comment + allow).
  * While qa-escalated set, skip suite re-run; reuse cached FAILED_CHECKS + runner.
  * At first cap hit (ITER reaches MAX_ITERATIONS), set qa-escalated + post J21 ESCALATED comment exactly once (idempotent via marker file).
  * Failure classification: classify_test_failure() distinguishes runner (exit 126/127, "command not found", "Cannot find module", testcontainers TypeError, etc.) from assertion. Runner path leads with "Test suite failed to run (environment/runner issue — fix the environment before changing code)".
- session-start.sh: surfaces qa-deferred tasks ABOVE qa-pending with a banner naming the auto-defer flow.

DOCS:
- docs/WORKFLOW.md label table gained qa-escalated, qa-deferred, qa-gate-entered, qa-blocked (full lifecycle).
- docs/HOOKS.md got a new "Escalation State Machine (spec 0.2)" subsection plus updated qa-gate.sh entry mentioning the `choose` subcommand.

TESTS (all green):
- L1: .claude/scripts/tests/qa-gate-choose.test.sh — 30 assertions covering usage errors, all four choose branches, and re-enter clearing qa-deferred/qa-escalated.
- L2: .claude/tests/component/specs/escalation-binding.sh — 42 assertions:
  * Iterations 1-3 invoke the test shim (count climbs to 3) and iter 3 sets qa-escalated + posts one comment.
  * Iter 4 blocks with escalation wording WITHOUT re-running the suite (shim count stays at 3).
  * Iter 5 auto-defers (qa-deferred set, {} envelope, qa-pending preserved).
  * Subsequent Stop with qa-deferred allows immediately.
  * Re-enter clears qa-deferred/qa-escalated and the next Stop re-runs the suite (count to 4).
  * Runner-vs-assertion classification: exit 127 lands on "Test suite failed to run"; exit 1 with assertion output lands on "Tests failing".
  * Counter reset on active-task change: TID2/TID3 hold independent counter files.

DOCS VERIFICATION (devops, 2026-06-11):

Enumeration mechanism (authoritative):
- API: GET https://api.anthropic.com/v1/models with header 'X-Api-Key: $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY' and 'anthropic-version: 2023-06-01'.

- No CLI listing (verified: 'claude --help' offers --model but no list subcommand).
- No publicly documented 'claude-opus-latest' style alias on the API response (only model-id versions).

Aliases in agent frontmatter and model: settings:
- Source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/model-config

Implementation choice:
- Enumerate via curl GET /v1/models when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set; cache 1h; rank via .claude/model-ranking (family order, version DESC, unknown-newer = same prefix higher version); prefer the '[1m]' variant where the listing distinguishes context window via max_input_tokens (we'll filter family-prefix matches and pick max max_input_tokens, suffixed with [1m] when supported).
- Fail-open paths: no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -> warn + keep pin; curl failure / timeout -> warn + keep pin; ranking empty -> warn + keep pin.

  - cmd_enter: sets rubric-pending alongside qa-gate-entered (fresh + idempotent paths); clears stale rubric-satisfied on re-enter; observations cite the rubric state.

Malformed input -> stdout JSON envelope with error_key naming the offender (missing_key:<k>, verdict_invalid_enum, criterion_results_not_array, iteration_not_number, invalid_json, empty_input, file_not_found, unknown_flag, etc.) + non-zero exit. On satisfied: posts comment "RUBRIC <version> iteration <n>: satisfied — all criteria pass"; removes rubric-pending; adds rubric-satisfied. On needs_revision: posts comment "RUBRIC <version> iteration <n>: needs_revision — failed: <names>"; labels unchanged (the qa-blocked round-trip is the QA agent's move per principle 7).
  - cmd_approve: NOT hard-gated on rubric-satisfied (principle 6). Warns loudly in observations when rubric-pending is still present (the agent's prompt in A.2 enforces the override-reason rule). Drops rubric-pending (cycle ends); preserves rubric-satisfied (audit trail).

- docs/WORKFLOW.md: label table gains rubric-pending + rubric-satisfied rows mirroring the 0.2 row style.
- .claude/scripts/tests/qa-gate-grade-record.test.sh: 87 assertions across 9 sections. Covers enter rubric-pending + re-enter clearing stale rubric-satisfied + idempotent refresh; grade-record satisfied (comment "RUBRIC v1 iteration 1: satisfied — all criteria pass" + label flip); grade-record needs_revision (comment with failed criterion names + labels unchanged + qa-blocked NOT auto-added); --file variant + nonexistent-file error_key; 9 malformed-input cases each asserting the structured error_key; approve-with-rubric-pending WARNING + still-succeeds + cycle ends; approve-with-rubric-satisfied audit-trail preserved; status rubric state across not-entered/entered/post-satisfied; structural sanity (frontmatter version: 1 on every rubric file, extends: default on domain files, applies_to: bug on overlay, iteration_cap=3 in config); META-TEST that awk-stubs the satisfied-branch label flip in a tempdir copy of qa-gate.sh and asserts rubric-satisfied is NOT set under the stub — proving the Section 2 assertion is sensitive to the production behavior, not vacuous.

IN PROGRESS: None — A.1 plumbing ready for QA. A.2 (grader.md + qa.md wiring + L2 loop spec) is the next child task.

KEY DECISIONS:
1. grade-record input shape: --file <path> OR stdin (tty-detected). Both forms exist so the QA agent can pipe (natural pattern) or attach a saved verdict for replay/debugging. tty detection prevents a hang when an operator types the subcommand alone.
2. Structured error envelopes via new emit_error_json (separate from emit_json) so the QA agent can branch on error_key without parsing prose. agent-centric per bd-mcp conventions.
3. Comment shape exactly matches spec wording: "RUBRIC <v> iteration <n>: <verdict> — <summary>" where summary is "all criteria pass" for satisfied OR "failed: <names>" for needs_revision (defensive: if needs_revision arrives without any failing criteria, summary cites required_fixes count instead).

5. Approve label policy: drop rubric-pending (cycle ends, regardless of verdict). Preserve rubric-satisfied if present (audit trail). Both consistent with the "labels are the record" thin-wrapper rule (principle 7).
6. Domain rubrics kept short (4 criteria each) per spec ("the grader's leverage is default + bugfix"). Each criterion is a checkable assertion with an "Evidence that satisfies it" line.
7. META-TEST mutates the production script via awk targeting two specific lines (anchored on the literal source shape) and verifies the mutation actually landed (META-TEST-of-META-TEST guard via grep count); shell-builtin `true` is used in the stub rather than /bin/true so the mutation parses on both macOS (/usr/bin/true) and Linux (/bin/true).

- make test-all: 8/8 L1 files pass + 18/18 L2 specs (315 assertions) pass. Standalone bash .claude/scripts/tests/qa-gate-grade-record.test.sh: Passed: 87, Failed: 0.

- Gate not approved (spec says do not approve).

title: Bug: bd-github-link.sh idempotency grep cannot find existing comment due to bold markdown mismatch
description: post_link_comment() in bd-github-link.sh writes a comment body of 'bd-link: tracked as **`<tid>`** (...)' (line 221, includes `**` bold markers) but the idempotency grep (line 234) looks for 'bd-link: tracked as `<tid>`' (NO bold markers). Because grep -F is fixed-string and the literal contains `**` between 'as ' and '`<tid>`', the grep never matches. Result: duplicate comments WILL be posted on every close — idempotency is broken. Reproduce: post a comment via the script, then run it again with the same task. Both runs will call gh comment. SUGGESTED FIX: change the grep pattern on line 234 to 'bd-link: tracked as **`<tid>`**' (match the actual body shape) OR change the body shape on line 221 to NOT use bold markers. Either keeps idempotency. SEVERITY: HIGH — duplicates pollute issue threads.
notes: FIXED: removed the bold markdown markers around the backtick-wrapped tid in gh_link_comment_body() (line 221, was 'tracked as **`tid`**', now 'tracked as `tid`'). The grep -F idempotency pattern in post_link_comment() (line 240, unchanged) now byte-aligns with the body. Added a comment block above gh_link_comment_body explaining the byte-alignment requirement so future edits can't drift again. Verified by 2 assertions in .claude/scripts/tests/bd-github-link.test.sh Section 3, including a stateful gh stub that proves '3 close fires => exactly 1 post'.

title: Phase 4: QA gate redesign
notes: IN PROGRESS: QA re-review started for Phase 4 fix pass

comments.author: username
comments.text:
QA-GATE BLOCKED at 2026-05-08T16:10:22Z: Phase 4 review found multiple defects contradicting the implementation report.

CRITICAL (must-fix):

1. bd update --status completed is invalid in Beads 0.47.1 (verify-before-stop.sh:258, :545). Valid value is closed. The || true swallows the error, so tasks never auto-close after QA approval. Self-hosting evidence: this very task is still in_progress with qa-gate-entered+qa-pending despite enter having succeeded.

2. epic-gate.sh files_changed_of second jq invocation is missing -R flag (epic-gate.sh:130). Result: always returns []. The shared-files subcommand reports my_files=[] even when notes contain a valid {"files_changed":[...]} block. The entire B2 file-intersection feature is non-functional. Reproduction: bash .claude/scripts/epic-gate.sh shared-files <id> on any task that has a JSON contract in notes.

3. rc=$? after if ! run_with_timeout always reports 0 under set -e (verify-before-stop.sh:303-308, 314-319, 326-331). The timeout-vs-fail distinction is silently broken. Failed test runs report 'Tests failing (exit 0)' regardless of actual exit code. Reproduction: synthesize an override .claude/test-cmd that exits 7; the block reason says exit 0.

4. get_current_task fallback to bd list --status in_progress | jq .[0].id (verify-before-stop.sh:91-92, post-edit.sh:32-33, intent-router.sh:23-24). When the helper file is empty (which happens when qa-gate.sh enter's best-effort write_current_task silently fails — exactly the case for THIS task), the fallback picks an arbitrary in_progress task. Defeats F3's single-source-of-truth promise and reintroduces the brittle 'first in_progress' anti-pattern Phase 1 was supposed to remove.

MATERIAL FEATURE GAPS:

5. Iteration counter leaks across tasks. Counter file is at a fixed path, not keyed by task_id; only approve clears it. If a user abandons task A at iter=3 and starts task B without approving A, B begins at iter=4. Implementation report explicitly claims 'Verify it doesn't leak across tasks (different task_id should reset)' — that test would fail. Reproduction: set iteration-count=3 with task A as current-task, switch current-task to B, fire vbs — counter becomes 4 not 1.

6. J21 decision-gate options only surface on FAILED_CHECKS path (verify-before-stop.sh:375-388). The QA-required path (technical checks passed but no QA approval yet) never includes the options, even at iter>=3. The plan calls for 'decision gate at the end: present options' — currently the more common case (clean tech-checks waiting on QA) never sees the options.

LESSER CONCERNS (recommended follow-ups):

7. intent-router.sh:53-62 only computes CURRENT_TASK inside if command -v bd && [ -d .beads ]. When bd is unavailable, the helper-file value is silently dropped, contradicting the F3 docstring. Move the F3 read out of the bd-conditional block.

8. prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh case '*orchestrator*' is over-broad — 'data-orchestrator-pipeline' or any other subagent name containing the substring would be blocked. Tighten the match to exact identifiers.

9. compute_intent_payload diff_summary contains literal newlines in jq pretty-printed output. The OUTER block-reason envelope handles this via jq -Rs, but when the LLM extracts the inner JSON code-block as text and parses it, control characters U+0000-U+001F in strings are technically invalid per RFC 8259. Use jq -c (compact) for the inner payload.

WHAT WORKS:

- Marker bypass remains closed.
- qa-gate.sh enter/status/approve/block flow + atomicity + rollback.
- F3 helper file when bd is initialized AND helper file is non-empty.
- detect-stack.sh polyglot detection across npm/pytest/go/cargo/maven/gradle/phpunit/rake/swift/dotnet/make + .claude/{test,lint,type}-cmd overrides.
- F1 doc-only fast path WITH a current-task auto-approves correctly.
- F1 doc-only WITHOUT a current-task and no other in_progress task falls through with QA-required block (but breaks when there's any other in_progress task — see Issue 4).
- All hook outputs produce valid JSON (post-edit, vbs, intent-router, prevent-orchestrator-edits, qa-gate).
- prevent-orchestrator-edits.sh correctly probes 7 candidate subagent paths and doesn't false-positive when data is missing.
- tech-debt.sh add appends rows cleanly, sanitizes pipe characters, creates header on first run, --bd-task spawns linked task.
- Approval call (this exact qa-gate.sh approve invocation) succeeds even after multiple Stop fires; F4 wipes iteration state cleanly.
- hooks.json and settings.json hooks blocks are identical and well-formed; PreToolUse anchored matcher correct.
- Iterative loop counter increments on Stop fires AND escalation block fires at iter>=3 (when on FAILED_CHECKS path).
- Epic gate check decision transitions (defer / pass / block) are correct.

DECISION: Block. Issues 1-4 are functional defects directly contradicting Phase 4 spec items. Issue 9 is a critical regression that silently breaks the most-frequent code path — every task approved via the gate stays in_progress because the bd update line uses an invalid status value. Issue 3 entirely defeats B2's purpose. Issue 7 hides timeout vs failure distinction in all checks. Issue 4 lets verify-before-stop pick arbitrary tasks when the helper file is empty — and the plugin's own Phase 4 task is currently in that state, so this is reproducible right now.

The implementation report's 'synthetic tests run, all pass' claim is incomplete; tests 4 ('iteration counter increments... ESCALATION + J21 options surface at iter 3') and 11 ('qa-gate.sh enter writes current-task') would have caught issues 9, 6, and 4 if they exercised the actual production code paths.

QA-GATE BLOCKED at 2026-05-08T14:42:12Z: J26 and J27 work is sound — but the F7 completion contract in qa.md deviates from the base schema established in backend.md and frontend.md, and the cross-review brief explicitly calls schema drift the most important consistency check.

Backend.md and frontend.md both ship the identical base schema:
  task_id, files_changed, tests_added, decisions, blockers, llm_observations

qa.md ships:
  task_id, approved, files_verified, issues_found, must_fix, suggested_followups, synthetic_tests_run, llm_observations

The brief stated: "QA contract may have additional fields ... but the base 6 fields must be identical across backend / frontend, and qas superset must include the same base." It listed renaming blockers → gating_issues as exactly the kind of deviation that warrants a block. The qa.md contract:
  - renames files_changed → files_verified
  - renames tests_added → synthetic_tests_run
  - drops decisions
  - drops blockers
That is four base-field deviations.

Must fix: restructure section 8 so the base six fields appear with their canonical names and semantics (with QA-specific notes — files_changed = files materially edited as part of the review, tests_added = synthetic tests authored, decisions = calls QA made during the gate, blockers = issues that triggered the block), and then layer the QA-specific fields (approved, issues_found, must_fix, suggested_followups, synthetic_tests_run) on top as the documented superset. synthetic_tests_run can stay alongside tests_added, since they capture different things.

What did pass: 8-module taxonomy (SECRETS → INJECTION → AUTH → CONFIG → DEPS → AI → MOBILE → DATA) is present and correctly intent-routed (line 71 reads the diff as a human would, not regex over filenames). Each module has both "look for" and "Fix" lines and they are concrete. Root-cause framework has all five steps in the right order (Capture, Reproduce, Isolate, Minimal fix, Verify and prevent) and is positioned as the prerequisite for qa-gate.sh block (line 99 and the closing line of section 5). WebFetch self-check sits in the gate-header at line 19. Frontmatter intact, code fences balanced, no emoji, no ALL CAPS prose (only legitimate module-name tokens and acronyms). C6 preserved.

Once the contract is restructured, re-enter the gate.
